title: Linux, ChromeOS, and Android Support
description: A community lab runs several Linux distributions, managed Chromebooks, personal Android work profiles, and kiosk devices with different package, account, storage, permission, update, and recovery models. Learners cumulatively produce cross-platform support comparison and runbook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Relate kernel, distribution, release, desktop, shell, package manager, repository, service manager, filesystem hierarchy, user, group, permission, and device state.
Use bounded commands and graphical tools to inspect identity, release, files, storage, memory, processes, services, logs, network, packages, permissions, and exit status.
Distinguish browser, user profile, local files, cloud files, Android and Linux environments, verified boot, management, update, recovery, and powerwash boundaries.
